abagora

Blog

Blog posts in table format, sorted from latest to oldest. Read left to right.

Disclaimer: I treat this as an online journal. My thoughts may be jumbled, my ideas may be unfinished, and my opinions will inevitably transform as I learn more and change as a person.

#11. Minimalism Will Make You Dangerously Focused

Tags: lifestyle
Minimalism is an art movement that evolved past its inception to become a philosophy that encompasses simplicity, moderation, and restraint.

#10. Addiction to Progression

Tags: addiction
MMO's are incentivized by the nature of their monetization system to keep you hooked.

#9. The Hierarchy of Progress

Tags: concept
he hierarchy of progress is a blueprint for what to do to achieve your goals, brick by brick, just like building a house.

#8. Action Leads to Insight

Tags: personal
In the twenty-first century, we are bombarded with information from the internet and real life.

#7. Stop Procrastinating with Note-Taking Apps

Tags: productivity
Stop procrastinating with Obsidian, Logseq, Notion, Roam.

#6. Time Equals Money

Tags: time-management
There is this saying that time equals money, when it refers to a business, but it can also be taken literally, as a way to save time on things you don't want to do.

#5. What Makes Time Valuable?

Tags: productivity
People talk about how you should spend your time 'wisely', but what does that really look like?

#4. Make Your Own Digital Garden

Tags: technology
A breezy way to tag your research or learning notes is with the digital garden system.

#3. Writing Down Flaws is Counterproductive

Tags: writing
It's bad to write down and constantly think about your flaws because you will think about them on a daily basis.

#2. Embrace Radical Incrementalism

Tags: productivity
Radical incrementalism is the idea that you should progress as slow as you want to in order to guarantee a high degree of quality in your work.

#1. Rule of Three For Books

Tags: reading
You should have an easy, medium, and difficult book to read.
